bullet_points: Load ic_final.npy and sim_metadata.npy.
Re-run the leapfrog integrator to generate 10 intermediate snapshots at t in {0.5, 1.0, ..., 4.5} for each of the 100 simulations.
Save raw intermediate snapshots to disk.
Compute and save virial radius and velocity dispersion per simulation as normalization coefficients.
Implement a utility to generate augmented (rotated/reflected) copies of snapshots on-the-fly for training.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Data Preprocessing, Normalization, and Snapshot Generation

bullet_points: Implement a standalone, importable Python module containing the GNN architecture.
Define classes for the interaction network and the force computation layer.
Enforce Newton’s Third Law in the edge function.
Incorporate the gravitational softening epsilon = 0.01 as a fixed physical prior.
Ensure the module is compatible with PyTorch device='cpu'.
Define system dynamics z_dot = f(z, theta) using the GNN module from Step 2.
Implement a Verlet-based (leapfrog) integrator within the Neural ODE framework.
Train on 80 simulations using intermediate snapshots, applying on-the-fly augmentation.
Use hyperparameter ranges: 50–100 epochs, hidden layers of size 64–128, learning rate 10^-3 to 10^-4, and a dynamic energy regularization weight schedule.
Save trained model weights and training/validation loss curves to disk.
Train a lightweight MLP (2-3 hidden layers) to map t=0 directly to t=5.
Evaluate the Symplectic Neural ODE (from Step 3) and the MLP baseline on the 20-simulation held-out test set.
Compute and plot energy drift over the predicted trajectory for both models.
Calculate final MSE metrics on positions and velocities in physical units using normalization coefficients from Step 1.
Train an ablation model (no softening kernel) using the same architecture and hyperparameters as Step 3.
Save ablation model weights.
Generate a side-by-side comparison table of MSE metrics (positions, velocities, energy error) for all three models: Neural ODE (with softening), Neural ODE (without softening), and MLP baseline.
Print and save all comparison statistics.
Synthesize results: training/validation loss curves, position/velocity MSE on test set, energy conservation error over time, and qualitative trajectory comparisons.
Interpret the performance of the Symplectic Neural ODE in capturing gravitational dynamics.
Discuss the impact of the softening kernel and the effectiveness of the symplectic integration approach.
Write the full Results section of the report, ensuring all quantitative results and plots from previous steps are referenced.
